The Iris Murdoch Society exists to promote her work, further her philosophical vision, and enhance and extend knowledge. You can find our website here: [irismurdochsociety.org.uk/](https://irismurdochsociety.org.uk/) You can find us on Twitter - [twitter.com/IrisMurdoch](https://twitter.com/IrisMurdoch) On Facebook - [www.facebook.com/groups/2213699051](https://www.facebook.com/groups/2213699051) And at Chichester University: [www.chi.ac.uk/humanities/public-humanities/literary-and-c](https://www.chi.ac.uk/humanities/public-humanities/literary-and-c) ... more

16 Apr 2026 • 59 min

### Comyns, Murdoch, du Maurier, and the Gothic Podcast

In this episode Miles discusses the mid-twentieth century gothic novel with a particular focus on Daphne du Maurier, Barbara Comyns and, of course, Iris Murdoch. An enduring subject of fascination, the gothic novel has undergone substantial change ov... more

Listen

4 Apr 2026 • 53 min

### Iris Murdoch and the Transcendent Podcast

In this episode Miles is joined by Jil Evans and Charles Taliaferro (St. Olaf College, Minnesota, USA) to discuss their new book, 'Iris Murdoch and the Transcendent'. We cover love, ethics, mora illumination, gender, vision and the will and much more... more

Listen

26 Mar 2026 • 56 min

### Iris Murdoch's Moral Philosophy Podcast

In this episode Miles is joined by Cathy Mason (Central European University, Vienna) to discuss her new book, 'Iris Murdoch's Moral Philosophy: Reframing the True, the Real, and the Good'. Host of The Iris Murdoch Podcast and affiliated with The Iris Murdoch Society; regular presenter and facilitator of discussions on Murdoch's philosophy and literature

Highly researched and nicely diversified audience and topics.

Thoughtful, compelling discussions that deepen understanding of Murdoch.

## Talking Points

Recent interactions between the hosts and their guests.

Iris Murdoch and the Transcendent Podcast

Q: Could you tell us a little bit about what inspired you to write it and what we can expect from it?

The authors describe a shared long-term engagement with Murdoch's work, aiming to provide an accessible introduction that also probes deeper questions about transcendence, beauty, and the good within Murdoch's philosophy and fiction.

Iris Murdoch's Moral Philosophy Podcast

Q: How does Murdoch's notion of truth differ from the dominant conception you describe, and why does that matter for contemporary philosophy?

Murdoch treats truth as a personal, ethically infused, and hierarchically nuanced concept rather than a binary, impersonal property of propositions; this shifts how we view realism, requiring(re)imagining what counts as truth and how moral perception informs our understanding of the world.

75th Episode Podcast

Q: How should Murdoch's philosophical seriousness be reassessed in light of recent scholarship on her as a public intellectual?

Murdoch's philosophical contributions are increasingly recognized and discussed, reflecting her importance as a public intellectual alongside her literary achievements.

Iris Murdoch's Wild Imagination Podcast

Q: What was the moment that convinced you that this needed to be a project that was going to be a PhD?

I felt it was incomplete. I had unanswered questions after my MA and sensed there was more to explore in the connection between fiction, philosophy, and poetry.

Iris Murdoch's Wild Imagination Podcast

Q: Lucy, tell us a little bit about the beginnings of the book. Where did your first interest in Murdoch and the Natural World come from?

I trace it back to when I started working with Anne Rowe at Kingston University in 2014 during my master's degree, where I noticed Murdoch's strong belief in the effective response to literature.

## Frequently Asked Questions About The Iris Murdoch Society podcast

### What is The Iris Murdoch Society podcast about and what kind of topics does it cover?

The show centers on Iris Murdoch's philosophy, literature, and the wider ethical and existential questions her work raises. Episodes feature in-depth conversations with scholars, critics, and practitioners who situate Murdoch within metaethics, virtue, narrative, and moral psychology, often contrasting her ideas with other philosophers. A notable strength is the range of expert guests—from university professors to researchers—providing rigorous yet accessible explorations of Murdoch's thought and its modern relevance. Listeners include philosophy enthusiasts, students, and professionals seeking thoughtful, academically grounded discussion tied to Murdoch's literary output and moral philosophy.
